summary: Dev Pod images (GPU + CPU) + dev R2 runtime channel, both gated on the EXISTING server-side _devMode (remotePodLifecycle.js:37). 1.1.0 is released and every released app pins the same image tag AND the same 'stable' runtime channel, so publish-runtime.sh stable reaches real users on their next Pod boot. No new Dockerfile, no CI change (manifest_version=0.17.0-dev already yields v0.17.0-dev-cu130). Safety substrate for MPI-333 / MPI-329 / MPI-341.

Moved to TOP of todo (user) - MPI-340 unblocks MPI-333/MPI-329 (both likely land fixes in wrapper.py, which currently publishes straight to the 'stable' channel released users boot). Brief extended: the dev/user split for wrapper.py + start.sh is the CHANNEL, not duplicate wrapper.dev.py files (git versions the file, the channel versions the deploy). Added a 'publish-runtime.sh promote' verb that rclone-copies vision/dev/* -> vision/stable/* server-side so promotion ships the EXACT bytes tested on dev, refusing on a working-tree/dev sha mismatch (the script already emits per-file sha256 in manifest.json). Runtime promote and image bump are independent paths.

Scope extended (user): the dev/stable split must land in docs, rules and skills, not just code. Grep-verified blast radius added to brief: CLAUDE.md:74 (Context Router row - highest-traffic instruction, currently teaches 'publish-runtime.sh stable' as THE way to ship a runtime edit), docs/runpod-remote-engine.md s5+s6, docs/builder/02-image-and-rebuild.md:100, .claude/commands/build-pod-image.md:123+94, .claude/rules/comfy_engine.md:291, docs/runpod-troubleshooting.md (4 mentions), mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/README.md 'Runtime externalize', plus a dos_and_donts.md one-liner. KEY FINDING: mpi-release/SKILL.md and mpi-version-bump/SKILL.md have ZERO Pod awareness today (grep-verified) - harmless with one channel, but this card creates the hazard, so mpi-release gains a Preconditions drift check (curl dev vs stable manifest.json, compare the sha256 fields they already carry) that STOPS and asks the user to promote or confirm deliberate non-shipping. Never auto-promote in the release flow - live op affecting released users, same class as git push. mpi-version-bump deliberately unchanged: app version and Pod runtime are separate artifacts on separate cadences.
todo -> doing: implementing dev Pod image tags + dev R2 runtime channel (dev_mode-gated) + publish-runtime.sh promote verb + the docs/rules/skills sweep.

Code + docs sweep SHIPPED. remotePodLifecycle.js: POD_IMAGE_VERSION_DEV/_CPU_DEV consts + _devMode branch in podImageForCard, and spec.env.CUBRIC_RUNTIME_CHANNEL='dev' on create (outside the noGpu branch, so CPU download Pods get it too). DEVIATION (deliberate): the dev image consts ship EQUAL to the stable pins - Docker Hub has no v0.17.0-dev tag, and an unbuilt tag 404s the pull so the Pod exits at boot (the v0.10.3-cpu trap). MPI-341 builds the first dev image, then moves the DEV consts. publish-runtime.sh gained dev|promote|stable modes: promote sha256-diffs the working tree against the live vision/dev/manifest.json, REFUSES on drift naming the file, then rclone-copies dev->stable server-side; stable prints a loud bypass warning; an unknown mode is a usage error. New promote-guard.test.sh covers both refusal paths + the pass path (PASS, no R2 writes). Resolver self-check in a vm with _devMode forced both ways confirms a non-dev build can only resolve the frozen pins. All 9 doc/rule/skill targets updated incl. the CLAUDE.md router row and the mpi-release Preconditions manifest-drift check (never auto-promote). Remaining: live Pod verify (dev GPU + dev CPU + portable-stays-stable), seed the dev channel with ./publish-runtime.sh dev, and one real promote. See validation.md.

Follow-up sweep (user): the dev/release split is now wired into the build-pod-image FLOW, not just two mid-flow asides - top blockquote (two image lines), step 1 asks DEV or RELEASE first and defaults to dev, step 4a dispatch passes manifest_version=<ver>-dev with BOTH legs (v0.10.3-cpu 404 trap), and the done-definition gains a dev variant (verify on a source-run Pod, never move the stable pins, never close the parent card). CONTRADICTION FIXED: the MPI-119 version guard regex only allowed ^X.Y.Z$, so it would have rejected the 0.17.0-dev input the same file told you to pass - now ^[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+(-dev)?$. Also corrected pre-existing drift in docs/runpod-remote-engine.md section 6 (the image CUDA-floor bullet still described the deleted -cu124/-cu128 split + per-arch volume sage compile; now single cu130 on nvidia/cuda:13.0.3-runtime, torch 2.12.0+cu130, flat allowedCudaVersions ['13.0'], SDPA-only). User has published the dev runtime channel. Commits 8349f7e2, 0c35cc9a.

LIVE VERIFIED (user, 2026-07-23): dev channel seeded and BOTH Pod flavors boot it. GPU Pod qjh6edvwx0bqav 08:43 and CPU download-mode Pod 96malfxl1cnqmp 08:44 both log 'fetching runtime from https://pod.cubric.studio/vision/dev (channel=dev)' with manifest channel=dev; the CPU Pod also fetched start-cpu.sh from vision/dev/ (that is the leg the outside-the-noGpu-branch placement buys, and the one that would have silently regressed), reached download mode, stamped manifest schema 2 / wrapper 0.2.37 and served /health 200. Baked CUBRIC_WRAPPER_VERSION unset on both = fetched wrapper self-reports. dev and stable manifests are byte-identical right now (wrapper 0.2.37 from MPI-276 reached stable before the split), so promote is a no-op today and the mpi-release precondition passes clean. STILL OPEN: (1) live proof that a released build resolves channel=stable (code-proven only; a pre-1.2.0 portable cannot prove it since it predates this code - real proof is a temporary fake BUILD_HASH stamp in js/core/buildInfo.js), (2) a real promote against R2 (refusal paths fixture-tested; the remote-to-remote copy has never run).

validation.md: added item 3 to 'Still NOT verified' - the dev IMAGE tag path is build-gated on MPI-342's dev-tag build (the DEV consts still sit equal to the stable pins, so a dev run has never pulled a different tag). Verification of that leg now lives in MPI-342's FINAL STAGE. Items 1 and 2 (fake BUILD_HASH released-resolves-stable proof, one real promote) are NOT build-gated and stay on this card.

MPI-342 phase 4 code half: POD_IMAGE_VERSION_DEV and POD_IMAGE_VERSION_CPU_DEV moved v0.16.0 -> v0.17.0-dev in routes/remotePodLifecycle.js, the first time that pair has pointed anywhere other than the stable pin. Both dev tags are built, pushed, public and pull-verified, so the unbuilt-tag 404-at-boot hazard does not apply. Stable pins untouched at v0.16.0; node --check passes. STILL UNPROVEN: the live half - create a Pod from a source run and confirm it pulls the -dev tag while a released run still resolves v0.16.0. Needs an app restart first (these consts bake into the Express child at boot).

MPI-340 build-gated leg PROVEN on the cpu half (live, 2026-07-23, Pod 3ln3y4anycuort). A source run pulled ghcr.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.17.0-dev-cpu ('Downloaded newer image', digest sha256:3e1b6add) - the first time the dev IMAGE tag path has resolved anything other than the stable pin. Same boot also proved the dev RUNTIME channel: '[cubric-bootstrap] fetching runtime from https://pod.cubric.studio/vision/dev (channel=dev)', manifest channel=dev wrapper_version=0.2.37, wrapper.py and start-cpu.sh both installed from the dev prefix. Volume compat confirmed live: '[cubric] manifest stamped (schema 2, wrapper 0.2.37)' on a pre-existing volume holding 3 models - schema 2 matches the app's MANIFEST_SCHEMA_MAX so the gate passes, and the stamp merges rather than wipes. Download mode confirmed: 'ComfyManager disabled (download mode / no GPU)', /health 200. Left for the GPU half: same proof on v0.17.0-dev-cu130, plus the released-run-resolves-v0.16.0 side.

Loose end CLOSED: the app-side WRAPPER_VERSION = '0.2.36' pin in routes/remotePodLifecycle.js is INERT on any R2-floated boot. The Pod log shows '[cubric-bootstrap] unset CUBRIC_WRAPPER_VERSION - fetched wrapper self-reports version', so the bootstrap deliberately clears the app's env var and the fetched wrapper reports its own version (0.2.37 in the manifest stamp). No app-side bump needed, and no released-user risk from the lag.

MPI-340 build-gated leg now FULLY PROVEN - GPU half live 2026-07-23 (Pod 5sn0x7l1my2rvz). A source run pulled docker.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.17.0-dev-cu130, 'Downloaded newer image', digest sha256:80351c10b7e7e991b5d5f426cc78041295ea8889335fda22200959ea93c8654c - byte-identical to what CI pushed and to the tag pull-verified locally, so the chain build->registry->RunPod is proven end to end on one digest. CUDA Version 13.0.3 confirms the cu130 base. Dev runtime channel fetched again (channel=dev, wrapper_version 0.2.37, wrapper.py from the dev prefix). Together with the cpu half earlier, BOTH legs of the dev IMAGE tag path have now resolved a real -dev tag rather than a tag equal to the stable pin - the branch MPI-340 could previously only ship half-proven. Still NOT build-gated and still open: the released-build-resolves-stable proof (fake a BUILD_HASH stamp) and one real promote run.

Item 1 (released-build-resolves-stable) PROVEN, no Pod. Ran the REAL _devMode IIFE bytes (eval-extracted from routes/remotePodLifecycle.js:37-44) against a faked release BUILD_HASH reading the actual on-disk js/core/buildInfo.js: dev stamp -> _devMode=true -> v0.17.0-dev tags + dev channel + 8188 open (matches the live-proven dev boots); faked hash a1b2c3d -> _devMode=false -> v0.16.0 stable tags + stable channel (env unset) + 8188 closed. Image tag + channel are pure load-time functions of _devMode decided app-side before any RunPod call, so no live Pod is needed. Proof only became meaningful once MPI-342 pointed the dev consts at real v0.17.0-dev tags (before that dev==stable). buildInfo.js restored to dev, git clean. Item 3 (dev IMAGE tag path) also closed via MPI-342 Phase 4 (both legs pulled the real -dev tags live). ONLY item 2 remains: one real publish-runtime.sh promote run - OUTWARD-FACING (writes stable = released users runtime), needs explicit user go, NOT run on a blanket continue.

Item 2 (one real publish-runtime.sh promote) DEFERRED by user decision 2026-07-23 - not shipping the dev runtime to released users yet. It is the sole remaining open item on MPI-340 (items 1 and 3 now proven). Outward-facing: promote copies dev bytes -> stable and every released v1.1.0 Pod boots it next start; there is no dry-run that does the real remote copy without the effect. Run when ready to actually ship: cd c:/AI/Mpi/mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod && ./publish-runtime.sh promote (guard refuses on working-tree drift vs live dev). Card stays doing/validating.
